# n-dx

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 474
- **Lines of code**: 125,015
- **Languages**: TypeScript (384), CSS (25), JSON (24), Other (14), JavaScript (12)
- **Zones**: 8 (8 with descriptions)
- **Git**: feature/from-recommend @ a67e98f
- **Analyzed**: 2026-02-10T03:30:53.703Z

## Architecture Zones

### Autonomous Agent System (`autonomous-agent-system`)

Complete autonomous execution engine with task lifecycle management, planning, tool dispatch, and validation workflows.

- Files: 80
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/autonomous-agent-system/context.md`

### Claude Integration Layer (`claude-integration-layer`)

Unified Claude API client abstraction supporting dual provider architecture (API and CLI modes) with automatic failover.

- Files: 15
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/claude-integration-layer/context.md`

### Orchestration Layer (`orchestration-layer`)

Top-level command router and tool coordination that delegates workflow commands to specialized packages.

- Files: 4
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/orchestration-layer/context.md`

### Rex PRD Management CLI (`packages-rex:rex-cli`)

A self-contained CLI tool and MCP server for managing hierarchical product requirements documents, including code analysis, tree operations, validation, persistence, and AI-agent workflow integration.

- Files: 36
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-rex:rex-cli/context.md`

### Analysis Engine (`packages-sourcevision:analysis-engine`)

Core analysis pipeline including file inventory, import graph extraction, Louvain community detection, route parsing, AI enrichment, and CLI orchestration.

- Files: 33
- Cohesion: 0.94, Coupling: 0.06
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/components.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/imports.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/inventory.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/zones.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/schema/data-files.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:analysis-engine/context.md`

### Interactive Viewer (`packages-sourcevision:interactive-viewer`)

Preact single-page application that visualizes analysis results through force-directed graphs, zone maps, route trees, and findings panels, consuming only schema types with zero analyzer dependencies.

- Files: 46
- Cohesion: 0.96, Coupling: 0.04
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/schema/v1.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:interactive-viewer/context.md`

### Test Fixtures (`packages-sourcevision:test-fixtures`)

Self-contained sample TypeScript project used as input data for integration and end-to-end tests, intentionally isolated with no imports from or exports to production code.

- Files: 7
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:test-fixtures/context.md`

### Test Suite (`packages-sourcevision:test-suite`)

Unit, integration, and end-to-end tests covering analyzer logic, schema validation, CLI workflows, and the serve command, with imports reaching into both analyzer and viewer zones.

- Files: 5
- Cohesion: 0.46, Coupling: 0.54
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/schema/validate.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:test-suite/context.md`

## Route Structure

### Route Tree

```
/ → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/_index.tsx
/login → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/_auth.login.tsx
/users/:id → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/users.$id.tsx
```

### Convention Exports

- action: 2 modules
- default: 4 modules
- loader: 2 modules
- meta: 1 modules

### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- Multi-package web server (with rex, hench, and sourcevision routes) is architecturally misplaced inside packages/sourcevision/ rather than at the orchestration layer where cross-package concerns belong. This creates artificial coupling and violates the clean three-layer architecture. [critical]

### Patterns

- Hybrid architectural pattern combines process-based orchestration with selective direct imports for performance-critical paths [info]

### Relationships

- Clean dependency inversion through claude-client shared foundation prevents circular dependencies between domain packages [info]

### Observations

- Contains 61% of project files (80/131) — subdivided into 5 sub-zones [info] (autonomous-agent-system)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (autonomous-agent-system)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (claude-integration-layer)
- Size imbalance: largest zone has 80 files vs smallest with 4 — uneven decomposition [info]
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (orchestration-layer)
- Perfect architectural isolation with no circular dependencies enables independent development and testing of each subsystem [info]

### Suggestions

- Inconsistent rex import patterns: static imports in some files, dynamic imports in task-lookup.ts - standardize approach [info] (autonomous-agent-system)
- Uses wildcard version 'rex': '*' instead of workspace protocol for local dependency consistency [info] (autonomous-agent-system)
- Inconsistent package naming: @n-dx/claude-client uses scoped naming while rex, hench, sourcevision use unscoped names [info] (claude-integration-layer)
- Inconsistent package export strategies across packages - standardize exports field usage for programmatic consumption [info]
- Mixed workspace dependency protocols: 'workspace:*' vs '*' wildcard - standardize to workspace protocol for local packages [info]
- Hardcoded tool paths in cli.js create brittle coupling to dist/ structure - consider using package.json bin field resolution [info] (orchestration-layer)

   Files: `ci.js`, `cli.js`, `config.js`
